As the array is sorted (but rotated), an O(log n) requirement highly suggests adapting Binary Search.
In any rotated sorted array, if you pick `mid`, at least one half (left to mid OR mid to right) will be strictly sorted.
First, find which half is properly sorted. You can check if the left half is sorted by comparing `nums[left]` with `nums[mid]`.

Once you know which half is sorted, check if your `target` lies within the boundaries of that strictly sorted half. If it does, discard the other half. If it doesn't, the target MUST be in the unsorted half.
